channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: MMEG Scholarships for Latin America 2026: Fully Funded Support for Women Advancing Education and Social Change
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
Applications are now officially open for the MMEG Scholarships for Latin America 2026, a prestigious funding opportunity designed to support women committed to improving the lives of women and children through education, leadership, and community impact. The Margaret McNamara Education Grants (MMEG) program provides financial assistance to women from developing and middle-income countries who are […]

The post MMEG Scholarships for Latin America 2026: Fully Funded Support for Women Advancing Education and Social Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now officially open for the MMEG Scholarships for Latin America 2026, a prestigious funding opportunity designed to support women committed to improving the lives of women and children through education, leadership, and community impact.

The Margaret McNamara Education Grants (MMEG) program provides financial assistance to women from developing and middle-income countries who are pursuing higher education and who demonstrate a strong commitment to social development and gender equality.

Women across Latin America who are currently enrolled in eligible universities can now apply for scholarship support for undergraduate, master’s, doctoral, and postdoctoral studies.

The application period runs from June 1, 2026, to September 30, 2026 (11:59 PM Eastern Standard Time).

About the MMEG Scholarship Program

The Margaret McNamara Education Grants (MMEG) is a non-profit organization established to support women pursuing higher education in order to improve the quality of life in their communities. The program specifically invests in women who are passionate about creating long-term social impact for women and children.

The scholarship aims to empower women leaders, researchers, educators, policymakers, and professionals who intend to contribute to sustainable development and community transformation after completing their studies.

Applicants selected for the scholarship become part of a global network of women leaders and changemakers working across multiple sectors and disciplines.

What the MMEG Scholarship Supports

The MMEG Scholarship supports women enrolled in:

  • Undergraduate/Pregraduate programs
  • Master’s degree programs
  • Doctoral programs
  • Postdoctoral studies

The scholarship is intended to help cover educational and academic-related expenses while students continue their studies at participating universities in Latin America.

Applicants are required to submit their estimated expenses in U.S. Dollars during the application process.

Application Deadline

  • Opening Date: June 1, 2026
  • Closing Date: September 30, 2026 (11:59 PM EST)

Applicants are strongly encouraged to apply early and prepare all supporting documents before the deadline.

Successful applicants will be notified individually in January 2027, while results will also be published on the official MMEG website in mid-January.

Eligible Universities in Latin America

Applicants must already be enrolled and registered — not merely accepted — at one of the following participating institutions:

Participating Universities

  1. Centro Agronómico Tropical de Investigación y Enseñanza (CATIE), Costa Rica
  2. FLACSO
    • Argentina
    • Costa Rica
    • Ecuador
    • El Salvador
    • Guatemala
    • Honduras
    • Mexico
    • Panama
    • Paraguay
    • Dominican Republic
  3. INCAE
    • Costa Rica
    • Nicaragua
  4. Universidad Austral, Argentina
  5. Pontifical Catholic University of Peru (Universidad Católica del Perú), Peru
  6. Universidad de Buenos Aires (UBA), Argentina
  7. Universidad de los Andes, Colombia
  8. Universidad Iberoamericana, Mexico
  9. Universidad Nacional Autónoma de México (UNAM), Mexico
  10. Universidad Nacional de Colombia, Colombia
  11. Universidad Rafael Landívar, Guatemala

Eligibility Criteria for the MMEG Scholarship 2026

To qualify for the MMEG Latin America Scholarship, applicants must meet all the following requirements:

Age Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Identify as women
  • Be at least 25 years old by the application deadline

Citizenship Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be citizens of a country eligible under the MMEG program

Enrollment Requirement

Applicants must:

  • Be currently enrolled and registered in an eligible academic program
  • Study either full-time or part-time
  • Take at least two courses per academic period

Applicants must already be actively studying at one of the participating universities listed above.

Degree Levels Accepted

Applicants can be enrolled in:

  • Undergraduate programs
  • Master’s programs
  • PhD/Doctoral programs
  • Postdoctoral programs

Academic Continuation Requirement

After receiving the scholarship, recipients must continue their enrollment during the January–July academic period of the following year.

Graduation Timeline

Applicants must:

  • Have an expected graduation date no more than three years after receiving the scholarship

Institutional Restrictions

Applicants must not have family ties to:

  • Employees
  • Spouses
  • Retirees

of the following organizations:

  • The World Bank
  • International Monetary Fund (IMF)
  • Inter-American Development Bank (IDB)
  • Members of the MMEG Board

Commitment to Social Impact

One of the strongest components of the MMEG Scholarship is its emphasis on community impact.

The scholarship specifically supports women who intend to:

  • Return to developing countries after graduation, or
  • Continue living in developing countries

to work toward improving the lives of:

  • Women
  • Girls
  • Children
  • Vulnerable communities

Applicants are expected to demonstrate leadership potential, social responsibility, and a long-term commitment to development and equality.

Important Application Requirement: AI-Generated Essays Not Allowed

MMEG has clearly stated that all application essays must be written independently by applicants.

The organization emphasizes authenticity and personal storytelling. According to the scholarship guidelines:

  • Essays generated using Artificial Intelligence (AI) tools will lead to disqualification.
  • Applicants are encouraged to share genuine personal experiences and use their authentic voice throughout the application.

This requirement makes it extremely important for applicants to prepare original essays and personal statements.

Application Checklist and Preparation Tips

Before starting the online application, applicants are advised to review:

  • Eligibility Criteria
  • Frequently Asked Questions (FAQs)
  • Application Checklist

Preparing these documents early can significantly improve the quality and completeness of the application.

Why the MMEG Scholarship Matters

The MMEG Scholarship is more than just financial support. It is an investment in women who are working toward educational advancement and societal transformation.

Recipients become part of an international network of women leaders dedicated to:

  • Education
  • Social justice
  • Gender equality
  • Economic development
  • Community empowerment

The scholarship also provides visibility and recognition for women pursuing impactful careers in academia, policy, development, healthcare, education, and social innovation.

Final Thoughts

The MMEG Scholarships for Latin America 2026 represent a major opportunity for women pursuing higher education while working toward meaningful social impact in their communities.

Women leaders, researchers, educators, and changemakers across Latin America are encouraged to apply and take advantage of this opportunity to advance their education and strengthen their contribution to society.

With a strong focus on women’s empowerment, community leadership, and educational advancement, the MMEG Scholarship continues to support the next generation of impactful women leaders across the region.

Learn more and apply

Click HERE to learn more and apply for the MMEG Scholarships for Latin America 2026.

Explore more opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post MMEG Scholarships for Latin America 2026: Fully Funded Support for Women Advancing Education and Social Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations University Science-Policy Visiting Fellow – Data Governance Fellowship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland (CHF 5,500 Monthly Stipend)
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
Researchers and policy professionals from the Global South now have an exceptional opportunity to contribute to global discussions on digital governance, development, and emerging technologies through the United Nations University (UNU) Science-Policy Visiting Fellowship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland. Hosted by the United Nations University Centre for Policy Research (UNU-CPR) in collaboration with the Geneva Science-Policy […]

The post United Nations University Science-Policy Visiting Fellow – Data Governance Fellowship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland (CHF 5,500 Monthly Stipend) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Researchers and policy professionals from the Global South now have an exceptional opportunity to contribute to global discussions on digital governance, development, and emerging technologies through the United Nations University (UNU) Science-Policy Visiting Fellowship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland.

Hosted by the United Nations University Centre for Policy Research (UNU-CPR) in collaboration with the Geneva Science-Policy Interface (GSPI), this prestigious six-month fellowship offers selected fellows the chance to conduct policy-relevant research, engage directly with international organizations, and influence global conversations on equitable data governance.

The fellowship is specifically designed to strengthen Global South participation in international science-policy processes while supporting research that contributes to sustainable development, digital inclusion, and equitable governance frameworks.

About the United Nations University (UNU)

The United Nations University serves as the academic and research arm of the United Nations system. For more than five decades, UNU has generated policy-relevant research and practical solutions to some of the world’s most pressing challenges.

With researchers and institutes operating across multiple countries, UNU supports evidence-based policymaking related to:

  • Sustainable development
  • Human security
  • Economic development
  • Science and technology
  • Peace and governance
  • Climate and environmental challenges

Through its global network, UNU collaborates with governments, UN agencies, research institutions, and policymakers to advance the Sustainable Development Goals (SDGs).

About UNU Centre for Policy Research (UNU-CPR)

The United Nations University Centre for Policy Research is one of the leading policy research institutes within the UNU system.

Headquartered in New York with an additional office in Geneva, UNU-CPR focuses on:

  • Innovative policy solutions
  • Evidence-based research
  • International cooperation
  • Multilateral governance
  • Sustainable development

The Geneva office was established to strengthen engagement with the international policy community and support collaboration between scientific experts and decision-makers.

About the Geneva Science-Policy Interface (GSPI)

The Geneva Science-Policy Interface is an independent platform dedicated to promoting science-informed policymaking.

Established by the University of Geneva with support from the Swiss Federal Department of Foreign Affairs, GSPI helps bridge the gap between:

  • Scientific institutions
  • Researchers
  • International organizations
  • Diplomats
  • Policymakers

The organization facilitates meaningful collaboration that enables scientific evidence to inform international policy decisions.

Fellowship Focus: Data Governance for Development

The selected fellow will work on a major international research initiative titled:

“Data Governance for Development: From Asymmetry to Equitable Stewardship.”

This project is being undertaken in collaboration with the Secretariat of the Commission on Science and Technology for Development (CSTD).

The fellowship will explore how inequalities in data ownership, access, infrastructure, and governance contribute to development challenges worldwide.

Particular attention will be given to:

  • Data governance frameworks
  • Digital cooperation
  • Inclusive technology policies
  • Development-focused digital transformation
  • Equitable access to digital resources
  • Protection of vulnerable and marginalized populations

The research will contribute directly to discussions taking place within the United Nations system regarding the future of data governance.

Why Data Governance Matters

As data becomes one of the world’s most valuable strategic resources, many developing nations face challenges related to:

  1. Limited data infrastructure
  2. Unequal access to digital technologies
  3. Insufficient governance capacity
  4. Dependence on external digital platforms
  5. Underrepresentation in global digital policymaking

The fellowship seeks to address these challenges by developing policy recommendations that promote:

  • Inclusive digital cooperation
  • Fair data governance
  • Sustainable development
  • Digital equity
  • Shared prosperity

The research will also examine how data collection practices may unintentionally reinforce inequalities affecting:

  • Women
  • Rural communities
  • Marginalized populations
  • Developing countries
  • Vulnerable social groups

Responsibilities of the Visiting Fellow

The selected fellow will undertake a broad range of research and policy activities.

1. Research and Analysis

The fellow will:

  • Develop an analytical policy report on equitable data governance.
  • Conduct research on global digital governance frameworks.
  • Examine data-related inequalities affecting developing nations.
  • Contribute to the CSTD issues note.
  • Develop forward-looking policy recommendations.
  • Analyze implementation efforts related to the Global Digital Compact.

2. Science-Policy Engagement

The fellow will:

  • Engage with Geneva-based science and technology stakeholders.
  • Collaborate with organizations such as:
    • UNCTAD
    • CSTD
    • UN entities
    • Research institutions
  • Deliver presentations and policy briefings.
  • Facilitate stakeholder engagement activities.
  • Organize one to two workshops involving diplomats, scientists, and civil society representatives.

3. Knowledge Development

The fellow will:

  • Produce an academic article based on fellowship research.
  • Contribute to internal knowledge-sharing activities.
  • Support the development of future fellowship initiatives.
  • Strengthen international science-policy collaboration.

Fellowship Benefits

Selected fellows will receive substantial support, including:

Financial Benefits

  • CHF 5,500 monthly remuneration
  • CHF 3,000 lump-sum allowance
  • Travel costs to Geneva covered
  • Return travel home covered
  • Visa support provided

Professional Benefits

  • Opportunity to work directly with United Nations entities
  • Access to international policy discussions
  • Exposure to global science-policy networks
  • Collaboration with leading researchers and policymakers
  • Experience influencing international development agendas
  • Opportunity to publish policy and academic research

Networking Benefits

  • Direct engagement with diplomats
  • Collaboration with UN experts
  • Access to Geneva’s international policy ecosystem
  • Participation in workshops and global discussions
  • Connections with leading development and technology professionals

Required Qualifications

Applicants should possess strong academic and professional backgrounds relevant to the fellowship.

Educational Background

A PhD is strongly preferred in areas such as:

  • Economics
  • Development Economics
  • Business Administration
  • Public Policy
  • Governance
  • Political Economy
  • Data Science
  • Artificial Intelligence
  • Quantitative Modeling

Candidates with a Master’s degree and significant relevant professional experience may also be considered.

Experience Requirements

Applicants should demonstrate:

  • At least three years of policy-relevant research experience.
  • Experience working on science and technology policy issues.
  • Strong analytical and writing skills.
  • Ability to communicate complex topics to policy audiences.
  • Experience working with diverse stakeholders.

Additional advantages include:

  • Familiarity with data governance frameworks.
  • Knowledge of digital development policies.
  • Understanding of internet governance processes.
  • Experience organizing workshops and stakeholder consultations.
  • Knowledge of challenges facing developing countries.

Language Requirements

Applicants must possess:

  • Professional-level fluency in English.

Additional UN languages are considered an asset.

Duration and Location

Fellowship Duration

  • Six months
  • Non-renewable

Location

  • Geneva, Switzerland

This is a fully in-person position requiring regular attendance in the office and participation in meetings and events.

Remote work is not permitted.

Application Process

Interested candidates must submit:

  1. A current CV in English (maximum two pages).
  2. A cover letter in English (maximum one page).
  3. Responses to position-specific application questions.

The cover letter should clearly explain:

  • Motivation for applying.
  • Relevant qualifications and experience.
  • Potential contribution to the fellowship.
  • Long-term career goals.

Important Application Deadline

Applications must be submitted no later than:

17 June 2026 at 6:00 PM Geneva Time

Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis, making early submission highly advisable.

Late applications will not be accepted.

Apply Here

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship provides a rare opportunity for Global South researchers and policy professionals to influence international discussions on one of the most important development challenges of the digital era.

By joining UNU-CPR and GSPI, fellows will gain access to world-class research networks, engage directly with international policymakers, and contribute to shaping global approaches to equitable data governance.

For professionals passionate about technology, development, governance, and international cooperation, the UNU Science-Policy Visiting Fellowship represents a unique pathway to advance both career growth and global impact.

For more information, visit the official website. 

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post United Nations University Science-Policy Visiting Fellow – Data Governance Fellowship 2026 in Geneva, Switzerland (CHF 5,500 Monthly Stipend) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: LEGO Student Worker, Mould Supply Planning 2026 in Denmark: Gain Hands-On Supply Chain Experience with a Global Brand
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
Are you a university student looking to gain valuable real-world experience in supply chain management, planning, operations, and data management while working for one of the world’s most recognized and innovative companies? The LEGO Group is inviting applications for its Student Worker, Mould Supply Planning position based in Billund, Denmark. This exciting opportunity allows students […]

The post LEGO Student Worker, Mould Supply Planning 2026 in Denmark: Gain Hands-On Supply Chain Experience with a Global Brand appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Are you a university student looking to gain valuable real-world experience in supply chain management, planning, operations, and data management while working for one of the world’s most recognized and innovative companies? The LEGO Group is inviting applications for its Student Worker, Mould Supply Planning position based in Billund, Denmark.

This exciting opportunity allows students to contribute directly to operational planning processes while gaining practical experience in supply chain functions that support the production and delivery of LEGO products worldwide. As part of the Mould Supply Planning team, successful candidates will work alongside experienced professionals, learn industry best practices, and develop skills that can accelerate their future careers.

If you are passionate about problem-solving, data analysis, planning, and collaboration, this position offers an exceptional opportunity to build your professional experience with a globally respected employer.

About The LEGO Group

The LEGO Group is one of the world’s leading toy manufacturers and a globally recognized brand known for creativity, innovation, learning, and play. Headquartered in Billund, Denmark, LEGO has inspired generations through products that encourage imagination, problem-solving, and creativity.

The company’s culture is deeply rooted in collaboration, diversity, innovation, and continuous learning. LEGO emphasizes employee wellbeing, professional development, flexibility, and creating an environment where individuals can thrive both personally and professionally.

Working at LEGO provides exposure to international teams, world-class processes, and a workplace culture that values curiosity, creativity, and teamwork.

About the Student Worker, Mould Supply Planning Role

The Student Worker position is part of LEGO’s Mould Supply Planning team, a dedicated group responsible for managing critical planning processes that ensure moulds, master data, and parts move efficiently throughout the company’s value chain.

The team plays an essential role in supporting production operations by ensuring that planning information remains accurate, updated, and aligned with organizational needs.

This role offers students practical exposure to:

  • Supply chain operations
  • Production planning
  • Data management
  • Cost tracking
  • Process optimization
  • Business operations
  • Cross-functional collaboration

The position is ideal for students seeking hands-on experience that complements their academic studies.

Key Responsibilities

As a Student Worker in Mould Supply Planning, you will contribute to several important operational activities.

1. Support Weekly Operational Planning Tasks

You will help ensure the accuracy of operational data used in planning and cost management processes. This includes supporting workflows related to mould economics and resource tracking.

Responsibilities include:

  • Monitoring operational planning data
  • Supporting cost-related reporting activities
  • Ensuring data consistency and accuracy
  • Assisting with information flow across systems

2. Update Master Data and BOM Changes

The role involves supporting updates to master data records and Bills of Materials (BOMs), helping maintain accurate planning information throughout the organization.

Tasks include:

  • Updating planning records
  • Supporting system data maintenance
  • Assisting with BOM modifications
  • Ensuring information accuracy

3. Assist with Delay Reports and Planning Updates

You will support planning teams by helping maintain and update reports that track project timelines and operational performance.

Key activities include:

  • Updating planning reports
  • Monitoring operational delays
  • Supporting planning documentation
  • Assisting with performance tracking

4. Provide Administrative and Meeting Support

The student worker will assist with departmental coordination activities.

This may include:

  • Preparing meeting materials
  • Supporting team events
  • Organizing planning documentation
  • Assisting with internal communication activities

5. Collaborate Across Departments

The role requires working with multiple stakeholders across the LEGO organization.

You will:

  • Communicate with colleagues from various departments
  • Support collaborative planning activities
  • Participate in team discussions
  • Contribute to process improvements

About the Mould Supply Planning Team

The Mould Supply Planning team consists of approximately ten dedicated professionals responsible for ensuring smooth planning processes across LEGO’s production ecosystem.

The team focuses on:

  • Mould cost management
  • Master data maintenance
  • Part planning
  • Process efficiency
  • Value chain optimization

Team members work together to ensure that moulds and production components are available when needed, supporting the timely delivery of LEGO products around the world.

The environment is collaborative, supportive, and focused on continuous improvement.

Who Should Apply?

LEGO is looking for motivated and analytical students who are eager to gain professional experience while continuing their studies.

Ideal candidates are individuals who:

  • Enjoy solving practical business challenges
  • Have strong attention to detail
  • Thrive in collaborative environments
  • Are comfortable working with data
  • Have an interest in operations and supply chain management
  • Can balance academic and professional responsibilities

Required Qualifications

Applicants should currently be pursuing a university degree in a relevant field.

Relevant disciplines include:

  • Supply Chain Management
  • Economics
  • Data Management
  • Business Administration
  • Operations Management
  • Industrial Engineering
  • Logistics
  • Related fields

Essential Skills

Successful candidates should demonstrate:

Analytical Skills

  • Ability to analyze information effectively
  • Strong problem-solving capabilities
  • Data interpretation skills
  • Logical thinking

Technical Skills

  • Proficiency in Microsoft Excel
  • Proficiency in Microsoft PowerPoint
  • General Microsoft Office knowledge
  • Ability to work with business systems and databases

Communication Skills

  • Strong written communication
  • Strong verbal communication
  • Ability to collaborate with diverse stakeholders
  • Professional interpersonal skills

Organizational Skills

  • Ability to manage multiple priorities
  • Structured working style
  • Attention to detail
  • Time management capabilities

Language Skills

Applicants must be fluent in English, both written and spoken.

Benefits of Working at LEGO

LEGO offers a wide range of benefits that support employee wellbeing, professional growth, and work-life balance.

Family Care Leave

Employees have access to enhanced leave options that support important family and life events.

Insurance Coverage

LEGO provides:

  • Life insurance
  • Disability insurance
  • Additional protection benefits

These programs help provide peace of mind and financial security.

Wellbeing Support

LEGO prioritizes employee wellbeing through:

  • Physical wellness resources
  • Mental health support
  • Community-building activities
  • Creative engagement opportunities

Employee Discount

From the first day of employment, colleagues gain access to LEGO’s generous employee discount program.

Performance Bonus Opportunities

Eligible employees may participate in bonus programs when organizational goals and performance targets are achieved.

Hybrid Working Environment

LEGO supports flexible work arrangements through its hybrid working model.

Employees generally work:

  • Approximately three days per week in the office
  • Remaining days remotely where role requirements allow

The exact arrangement will be discussed during recruitment.

Diversity and Inclusion at LEGO

The LEGO Group is committed to creating an inclusive workplace where everyone feels respected, valued, and empowered.

Applications are encouraged from individuals of all backgrounds, regardless of:

  • Gender
  • Gender identity
  • Race
  • Ethnicity
  • National origin
  • Religion
  • Disability
  • Sexual orientation
  • Age

LEGO believes diverse perspectives strengthen innovation and contribute to organizational success.

Application Process

Applications are reviewed on an ongoing basis.

LEGO encourages interested students to apply as early as possible because positions may be filled before any advertised closing date.

Candidates should prepare:

  1. Updated resume or CV.
  2. Academic information.
  3. Relevant skills and experience details.
  4. Supporting application documents requested through the LEGO Careers portal.

Apply Here

Why This Opportunity Matters

For students interested in supply chain management, planning, operations, logistics, and business analytics, this role provides a valuable opportunity to gain experience with a globally recognized organization.

Working within LEGO’s Mould Supply Planning team allows students to develop practical skills, build professional networks, learn from experienced professionals, and gain insight into how a major international company manages complex operational processes.

Students seeking meaningful professional experience while completing their studies should strongly consider applying for this opportunity.

For more information, visit the official website. 

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

 

The post LEGO Student Worker, Mould Supply Planning 2026 in Denmark: Gain Hands-On Supply Chain Experience with a Global Brand appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: elea Talent Program 2027 in South Africa: Fully Immersive Global Career Opportunity in Impact Investing for University Graduates
item.dc:creator: Ghai A. Deng
item.description:
The elea Talent Program (eTP) 2027 is now accepting applications from ambitious and talented university graduates who are eager to build a meaningful career in impact investing, entrepreneurship, and international development. Organized by the elea Foundation for Ethics in Globalization, this prestigious two-year professional development program offers participants the opportunity to work alongside experienced investment […]

The post elea Talent Program 2027 in South Africa: Fully Immersive Global Career Opportunity in Impact Investing for University Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The elea Talent Program (eTP) 2027 is now accepting applications from ambitious and talented university graduates who are eager to build a meaningful career in impact investing, entrepreneurship, and international development. Organized by the elea Foundation for Ethics in Globalization, this prestigious two-year professional development program offers participants the opportunity to work alongside experienced investment professionals, gain exposure to entrepreneurial ecosystems across multiple continents, and contribute to solutions that address poverty and social challenges through business innovation.

For the 2027 intake, elea is launching its first South African cohort, marking a major milestone in the organization’s global expansion strategy. The selected candidates will join the program in January 2027 and be based in Johannesburg, South Africa.

If you are passionate about entrepreneurship, investment, innovation, and creating measurable social impact, the elea Talent Program could be the ideal stepping stone to an international career in impact investing.

What is the elea Talent Program?

The elea Talent Program is a structured two-year career development initiative designed to equip talented graduates with the skills, knowledge, and practical experience needed to thrive in the world of impact investing.

Participants join elea as Associates and gain hands-on exposure to the organization’s investment activities, entrepreneurial support programs, and operational functions. Throughout the program, Associates work closely with startups, investors, and entrepreneurs while receiving continuous mentorship, coaching, and professional development support.

The program provides a unique opportunity to understand how investments can be used as tools to create sustainable economic development and reduce poverty in emerging markets.

About elea Foundation for Ethics in Globalization

The elea Foundation for Ethics in Globalization is a Switzerland-based organization dedicated to fighting absolute poverty through entrepreneurship and impact investing. The organization supports promising early-growth companies that generate both financial sustainability and positive social impact.

By investing in entrepreneurs who solve critical societal challenges, elea helps create jobs, improve livelihoods, and foster economic development in underserved regions around the world.

The launch of the South African Talent Program cohort reflects elea’s commitment to developing local talent while strengthening its ability to identify and support impactful entrepreneurial solutions across Africa.

Why the elea Talent Program Stands Out

Unlike traditional graduate programs that focus on a single department or function, the elea Talent Program offers comprehensive exposure to the entire impact investing ecosystem.

Participants gain practical experience across multiple aspects of elea’s operations, allowing them to develop a broad understanding of how investments, entrepreneurship, and social impact intersect.

The program combines:

  • Professional work experience
  • International exposure
  • Mentorship and coaching
  • Skills development
  • Leadership growth
  • Cross-functional learning
  • Global networking opportunities

Key Development Goals of the Program

The elea Talent Program is specifically designed to help participants build critical professional competencies.

1. Company Assessment Skills

Participants learn how to evaluate businesses by analyzing:

  • Business models
  • Financial performance
  • Market opportunities
  • Growth potential
  • Social impact outcomes

2. Understanding Investment Mechanisms

Associates gain exposure to various investment approaches and learn how impact capital can be deployed to support entrepreneurial ventures.

3. Professional Skills Development

The program emphasizes both technical and interpersonal skills through:

  • Structured training programs
  • On-the-job learning
  • Professional coaching
  • Mentorship sessions
  • Practical assignments

4. Exposure to All Areas of elea Operations

Participants develop a comprehensive understanding of:

  • Investment processes
  • Entrepreneur support
  • Due diligence
  • Portfolio management
  • Impact measurement
  • Organizational operations

5. Personal Growth and Career Development

The program enables participants to discover their strengths and identify the specialization path that best aligns with their long-term career goals.

International Exposure and Travel Opportunities

One of the most attractive features of the elea Talent Program is its international dimension.

Participants may have opportunities to travel within:

  • Africa
  • Asia
  • Latin America
  • Switzerland

This global exposure allows Associates to learn directly from entrepreneurs, understand diverse markets, and develop a broader perspective on international business and impact investing.

Who Should Apply?

The elea Talent Program seeks highly motivated graduates who want to create meaningful impact through their professional careers.

Ideal candidates are individuals who:

  • Want to dedicate their talents to meaningful work
  • Are interested in entrepreneurship and innovation
  • Enjoy solving complex challenges
  • Thrive in collaborative environments
  • Demonstrate ambition and commitment
  • Are eager to learn and grow professionally
  • Have a global mindset
  • Are open to international travel

Eligibility Requirements

To be considered for the 2027 cohort, applicants should meet the following requirements:

Academic Qualifications

Applicants must possess:

  • A Bachelor’s degree or Honours degree
  • An above-average academic record

Professional Competencies

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Excellent analytical skills
  • Strong quantitative problem-solving abilities
  • Ability to work effectively in teams
  • Strong communication skills
  • Intellectual curiosity
  • Leadership potential

Language Requirements

Applicants must have:

  • Strong English language proficiency

Additional African languages are considered an advantage.

Work Authorization

Candidates must possess:

  • A valid South African work permit

Location Requirement

Applicants must either:

  • Be based in Johannesburg, South Africa, or
  • Be willing to relocate to Johannesburg

Leadership Experience

While not mandatory, preference may be given to candidates who have demonstrated leadership through:

  • Academic activities
  • Professional experiences
  • Community initiatives
  • Extracurricular involvement

Benefits of the elea Talent Program

Participants selected for the program gain access to numerous professional and personal development opportunities.

Benefits include:

  • Two years of structured professional development
  • Direct exposure to impact investing
  • International work experience
  • Travel opportunities across multiple continents
  • Training and coaching from industry professionals
  • Continuous mentorship and support
  • Experience working with innovative entrepreneurs
  • Development of analytical and investment skills
  • Access to global networks
  • Career advancement opportunities
  • Exposure to early-growth ventures with measurable social impact

Application Deadline

The application window for the 2026 recruitment cycle is currently open.

Application Deadline: 28 June 2026

Interested candidates are encouraged to submit their applications before the deadline to maximize their chances of consideration.

Apply Here

Why Graduates Should Not Miss This Opportunity

The elea Talent Program offers far more than a traditional graduate role. It provides participants with a rare opportunity to build expertise in impact investing while contributing to solutions that address some of the world’s most pressing social and economic challenges.

Graduates who aspire to work at the intersection of finance, entrepreneurship, and social impact will find the program particularly valuable. The combination of international exposure, professional development, mentorship, and meaningful work creates an exceptional foundation for a long-term career in the impact investing sector.

For talented graduates seeking a purpose-driven career with global opportunities, the elea Talent Program 2027 represents an outstanding opportunity to learn, grow, and create lasting impact.

For more information, visit the official website.

Discover more global job opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post elea Talent Program 2027 in South Africa: Fully Immersive Global Career Opportunity in Impact Investing for University Graduates appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa Women Writers Residency 2026: Fully Funded 4-Week Creative Writing Program for Emerging African Women Writers in Nairobi, Kenya
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The Africa Women Writers Residency 2026 is a fully funded literary development opportunity designed to empower emerging African women storytellers. This prestigious residency provides selected writers with the space, mentorship, training, and publishing platform needed to refine their craft and amplify their voices on an international stage. Organized by Majesty Publishing House Africa, the program […]

The post Africa Women Writers Residency 2026: Fully Funded 4-Week Creative Writing Program for Emerging African Women Writers in Nairobi, Kenya appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Africa Women Writers Residency 2026 is a fully funded literary development opportunity designed to empower emerging African women storytellers. This prestigious residency provides selected writers with the space, mentorship, training, and publishing platform needed to refine their craft and amplify their voices on an international stage.

Organized by Majesty Publishing House Africa, the program aims to address the long-standing barriers that many African women writers face, including limited access to mentorship, publishing opportunities, and global literary networks.

This initiative culminates in a published anthology titled “New African Voices: Stories That Shape Africa’s Future”, showcasing the works of selected participants.

 

About the Africa Women Writers Residency 2026

The residency is a structured creative writing development program running in September 2026. It is designed for twelve selected emerging women writers from across Africa.

The program combines:

  • Online intensive workshops and mentorship sessions
  • Guest lectures by established African authors
  • A fully funded in-person residency experience in Nairobi, Kenya
  • Publication of selected works in a professional anthology

The program supports writers working in multiple literary genres including:

  • Fiction
  • Fantasy
  • Creative nonfiction
  • Memoir writing
  • Poetry

This initiative is not only a training program but also a publication platform aimed at elevating African women’s literary voices globally.

 

Program Structure and Format

The residency is divided into two main phases:

1. Virtual Residency (3 Weeks)

Participants will engage in:

  • Interactive writing workshops
  • One-on-one mentorship sessions
  • Literary discussions and critiques
  • Guest lectures from established authors
  • Writing development exercises focused on thematic storytelling

2. In-Person Residency (1 Week in Nairobi, Kenya)

Selected writers will travel to Nairobi for an immersive experience that includes:

  • Intensive writing and revision sessions
  • Cultural exchange activities
  • Collaborative storytelling workshops
  • Networking with African literary professionals
  • Final editing and anthology preparation

All costs are fully covered, ensuring equal access for selected participants.

 

Funding and Support Coverage

This is a fully funded residency. Selected participants will receive:

  • International and local travel coverage
  • Accommodation during the in-person residency
  • Meals throughout the program
  • Local transportation in Nairobi
  • Cultural and learning activities
  • Mentorship and publishing support

This makes it one of the most accessible and impactful writing residencies for African women writers.

 

Eligibility Criteria

The program is open to emerging women writers from selected African countries.

Eligible Countries:

  • Kenya
  • Uganda
  • Ghana
  • Nigeria
  • South Africa

Eligible Applicants:

Applicants must:

  • Identify as women writers
  • Be emerging writers (published or unpublished)
  • Not currently have literary representation
  • Demonstrate proficiency in English
  • Be able to commit fully to the program duration

Eligible Writing Categories:

  • Fiction
  • Poetry
  • Creative nonfiction
  • Memoir
  • Fantasy writing

 

Application Requirements

Interested applicants must submit the following:

1. Motivation Letter (250 words)

Applicants must introduce themselves and explain:

  • Their writing journey
  • Their motivation for joining the residency
  • Their goals as writers
  • Their interest in African storytelling

2. Writing Sample (Theme-Based)

Applicants must respond to the theme:

“A New Dawn for Africa”

Submission options include:

  • A short story or essay (up to 1,000 words), OR
  • One poem (up to 1,000 words), OR
  • Two poems totaling approximately 1,000 words

 

Why This Residency Matters

The Africa Women Writers Residency plays a critical role in strengthening African literature by addressing structural barriers faced by women writers.

Key benefits include:

  • Professional literary mentorship
  • Exposure to global publishing opportunities
  • Development of storytelling skills
  • Access to an African literary network
  • Publication in a curated anthology
  • Increased visibility for emerging writers

By investing in women storytellers, the program ensures that African narratives are told authentically and reach global audiences.

 

Program Location

  • In-person residency location: Nairobi, Kenya
  • Virtual participation: Open to selected candidates across eligible countries

 

Deadline

  • Application Deadline: 22 June 2026
  • Applications must be submitted via email before the deadline: residency@majestypublishingafrica.com

 

Conclusion

The Africa Women Writers Residency 2026 is a powerful opportunity for emerging African women writers to develop their craft, gain mentorship, and achieve publication in a professional anthology. With full funding and strong editorial support, this residency offers a rare chance to transform creative passion into professional literary success.

Writers interested in participating should submit their materials to residency@majestypublishingafrica.com before the deadline.

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here 
  • More global Short courses opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here

The post Africa Women Writers Residency 2026: Fully Funded 4-Week Creative Writing Program for Emerging African Women Writers in Nairobi, Kenya appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: EuropeAid Grant 2026 in Zimbabwe: €6 Million Funding to Strengthen Civil Society, Local Governance and Sustainable Local Economies (EuropeAid/187085/DD/ACT/ZW)
item.dc:creator: Diakaridia KONE
item.description:
The European Union has launched a major funding opportunity aimed at strengthening civil society organizations and improving local governance systems while promoting sustainable local economic development in Zimbabwe. This initiative, under the EuropeAid framework, provides substantial financial support for impactful projects that empower communities, enhance accountability, and drive inclusive economic growth at the local level. […]

The post EuropeAid Grant 2026 in Zimbabwe: €6 Million Funding to Strengthen Civil Society, Local Governance and Sustainable Local Economies (EuropeAid/187085/DD/ACT/ZW) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The European Union has launched a major funding opportunity aimed at strengthening civil society organizations and improving local governance systems while promoting sustainable local economic development in Zimbabwe. This initiative, under the EuropeAid framework, provides substantial financial support for impactful projects that empower communities, enhance accountability, and drive inclusive economic growth at the local level.

With a total budget of €6,000,000, this action grant is one of the most significant funding opportunities available for civil society actors working in governance, democracy support, and sustainable development in Sub-Saharan Africa.

This opportunity is officially open for submission and targets organizations capable of designing and implementing high-impact, community-driven development projects.

 

About the EuropeAid Funding Programme

The initiative is funded under the European Union’s external cooperation instruments managed by EuropeAid. EuropeAid is responsible for designing and implementing development cooperation programs that support sustainable development, poverty reduction, governance reform, and institutional strengthening across partner countries.

This specific call, EuropeAid/187085/DD/ACT/ZW, focuses on Zimbabwe and aligns with broader EU development priorities in Sub-Saharan Africa.

The program aims to strengthen:

  • Democratic governance at local levels
  • Capacity of civil society organizations
  • Economic resilience in communities
  • Transparency and accountability in public administration
  • Sustainable and inclusive local economies

 

Objective of the Grant

The core objective of this funding call is:

To promote civil society contributions to good local governance and sustainable local economies in Zimbabwe.

This involves supporting initiatives that enable citizens, civil society organizations, and local institutions to work together more effectively in addressing governance challenges and promoting economic opportunities.

 

Key Areas of Intervention

Successful proposals under this grant are expected to address one or more of the following priority areas:

1. Strengthening Civil Society Organizations

  • Capacity building for NGOs and community-based organizations
  • Enhancing advocacy and policy engagement skills
  • Supporting civic participation and citizen engagement
  • Promoting organizational sustainability

2. Improving Local Governance Systems

  • Enhancing transparency and accountability mechanisms
  • Strengthening public service delivery at local level
  • Supporting participatory governance processes
  • Encouraging collaboration between local authorities and civil society

3. Promoting Sustainable Local Economies

  • Supporting local entrepreneurship and SMEs
  • Encouraging inclusive economic growth models
  • Promoting green and sustainable economic practices
  • Enhancing value chains at community level

4. Social Inclusion and Community Empowerment

  • Empowering youth and women in decision-making
  • Supporting marginalized and vulnerable groups
  • Strengthening community resilience
  • Promoting equitable access to resources

 

Funding Details

This call offers significant financial support for eligible applicants:

  • Total Budget: €6,000,000
  • Type of Funding: Action Grants
  • Geographic Focus: Zimbabwe (Sub-Saharan Africa)

Grants are expected to support multi-year interventions with measurable development impact.

 

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must design projects aligned with EU development priorities and demonstrate strong implementation capacity.

Eligible Applicants Include:

  • Civil society organizations (CSOs)
  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • International NGOs
  • Community-based organizations
  • Development-focused consortiums
  • Non-profit institutions working in governance and development

Required Capabilities:

  • Proven experience in governance or development projects
  • Strong financial and project management systems
  • Ability to operate in Zimbabwe or Sub-Saharan Africa
  • Strong monitoring and evaluation frameworks
  • Partnerships with local stakeholders

 

Expected Impact

Projects funded under this initiative are expected to deliver:

  • Improved governance transparency and accountability
  • Stronger participation of citizens in decision-making
  • Increased effectiveness of civil society organizations
  • Enhanced local economic resilience
  • Sustainable community development outcomes

 

Application Process

Interested organizations must submit their proposals through the official EU submission platform.

Steps include:

  1. Reviewing the full call document
  2. Preparing concept notes and full proposals
  3. Developing implementation frameworks
  4. Submitting applications via the EU submission service

 

Deadline

  • Submission Deadline: 21 July 2026

Late submissions will not be accepted.

 

Official Reference

  • Programme: Sub-Saharan Africa
  • Call Reference: EuropeAid/187085/DD/ACT/ZW
  • Status: Open for Submission

 

Why This Grant Matters

This EuropeAid funding opportunity is critical because it strengthens the foundation of democratic governance and sustainable economic development at the grassroots level. It empowers civil society organizations to act as catalysts for change, ensuring that communities have stronger voices in shaping policies and economic opportunities.

By investing in governance and local economies, the EU aims to build long-term resilience, reduce inequality, and promote inclusive development in Zimbabwe.

Apply here  

 

Learn More

  • For more information about this opportunity, Click here
  • More global Grant opportunities for youth, visit the OFY website: Click here 

The post EuropeAid Grant 2026 in Zimbabwe: €6 Million Funding to Strengthen Civil Society, Local Governance and Sustainable Local Economies (EuropeAid/187085/DD/ACT/ZW) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Cyber-Defence Doctoral Fellowships 2026 in Switzerland: Fully Funded Cyber-Defence PhD Opportunity for International Researchers
item.dc:creator: Isaac O
item.description:
The CYD Doctoral Fellowships 2026 in Switzerland are now open for applications, offering exceptional doctoral researchers around the world the opportunity to pursue cutting-edge research in cyber-defence, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, data science, and digital trust technologies. Hosted through the Cyber-Defence (CYD) Campus and coordinated by EPFL (École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne), this prestigious fellowship provides […]

The post Cyber-Defence Doctoral Fellowships 2026 in Switzerland: Fully Funded Cyber-Defence PhD Opportunity for International Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The CYD Doctoral Fellowships 2026 in Switzerland are now open for applications, offering exceptional doctoral researchers around the world the opportunity to pursue cutting-edge research in cyber-defence, cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, data science, and digital trust technologies. Hosted through the Cyber-Defence (CYD) Campus and coordinated by EPFL (École Polytechnique Fédérale de Lausanne), this prestigious fellowship provides up to four years of funding, research support, conference funding, and access to one of Europe’s leading innovation ecosystems.

Designed for highly talented doctoral candidates, the fellowship supports groundbreaking interdisciplinary research that contributes to strengthening cyber-defence capabilities and addressing emerging digital security challenges. Researchers from any nationality are eligible to apply, making this one of the most attractive international PhD funding opportunities in cybersecurity and defence-related technologies.

What is the CYD Doctoral Fellowship?

The CYD Doctoral Fellowship is a competitive funding programme aimed at supporting doctoral students conducting research at the intersection of cyber-defence, security, data science, and emerging technologies.

The fellowship enables selected candidates to complete their PhD research while benefiting from collaboration between the CYD Campus and Swiss higher education institutions. Beyond financial support, the programme fosters innovation, interdisciplinary cooperation, and impactful research that contributes to strengthening Switzerland’s cyber-defence ecosystem.

The initiative is specifically designed to encourage talented researchers to develop solutions addressing modern cybersecurity threats, technological vulnerabilities, digital trust issues, and challenges affecting national and international security.

Fellowship Mission and Research Focus

The programme seeks researchers willing to tackle complex cyber-defence challenges through innovative and interdisciplinary approaches.

Research proposals should contribute significantly to one or more of the following priority areas:

1. Cyber and Cyber-Physical Systems Security

Projects may focus on:

  • Protecting confidentiality of digital systems
  • Ensuring system integrity
  • Improving availability and resilience of cyber infrastructures
  • Securing critical cyber-physical systems

2. Information Sharing and Data Fusion

Research may include:

  • Secure information exchange
  • Collaborative intelligence systems
  • Crowdsourcing technologies
  • Data integration frameworks

3. Data Science and Artificial Intelligence

Applicants may explore:

  • AI-driven cybersecurity solutions
  • Machine learning applications
  • Predictive analytics
  • Cyber threat intelligence
  • Advanced data analysis techniques

4. Security, Privacy, and Digital Trust

Research topics may include:

  • Privacy-preserving technologies
  • Digital identity systems
  • Trustworthy computing
  • Secure digital environments
  • Privacy-enhancing frameworks

Expected Research Impact

Applicants must demonstrate that their proposed research:

  • Can significantly influence the fields of security and data science.
  • Has the potential to generate meaningful impact on Swiss cyber-defence capabilities.
  • Encourages collaboration between the CYD Campus and at least one Swiss higher education institution.
  • Advances knowledge and practical solutions within cybersecurity and digital security domains.

Financial Benefits of the CYD Doctoral Fellowship 2026

One of the strongest features of this programme is its comprehensive financial support package.

Selected fellows will receive:

Salary Support

  • Full salary contribution according to the salary scale of the host institution.
  • Coverage includes applicable social security benefits.

Research Funding

  • Up to CHF 3,000 per year for eligible research-related expenses.
  • Funding can be used for specific equipment required for the project.

Conference Funding

  • Up to CHF 5,000 per year for:
    • Conference participation
    • Academic presentations
    • Research dissemination activities
    • International networking opportunities

Fellowship Duration

  • Four years of support.
  • Possible one-year extension in eligible circumstances.

This funding structure allows doctoral candidates to focus on high-impact research while minimizing financial barriers.

Who Can Apply?

The CYD Doctoral Fellowships welcome applications from researchers across the globe.

Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be citizens of any nationality.
  • Have no age restrictions.
  • Be enrolled in a doctoral programme at a Swiss higher education institution.
  • Obtain endorsement from a professor at a Swiss higher education institution willing to supervise their work.
  • Demonstrate academic excellence and strong research potential.
  • Submit research proposals aligned with cyber-defence and security priorities.

Additional Eligibility Notes

Applicants may:

  • Apply at any stage of their doctoral studies.
  • Request funding for less than four years if their PhD completion timeline requires a shorter funding period.

Preference will be given to:

  • Candidates who have not yet started their PhD.
  • Researchers at the early stages of doctoral studies.

Applicants seeking endorsement from a professor at a Swiss University of Applied Sciences must also provide confirmation of enrolment in a doctoral programme at a Swiss university.

Two-Stage Selection Process

The CYD Doctoral Fellowship follows a rigorous two-stage evaluation process.

Stage 1: Initial Screening

Applications are reviewed by a CYD Campus internal committee based on:

  • Candidate suitability
  • Academic excellence
  • Research potential
  • Alignment with programme objectives
  • Relevance of the proposed project

Mentor Assignment

Successful Stage 1 applicants will:

  • Be assigned a prospective CYD Campus mentor.
  • Participate in an interview.
  • Receive mentor endorsement before progressing.

Stage 2: Full Proposal Submission

Endorsed candidates will be invited to submit:

  • A comprehensive research proposal.
  • Detailed project documentation.

Applications will then undergo final evaluation by a committee consisting of representatives from:

  • CYD Campus
  • Swiss higher education institutions
  • Leading experts in relevant fields

Important Dates

Stage 1 Application Deadline

19 August 2026 (17:00 CEST)

Stage 2 Full Proposal Deadline

1 October 2026 (17:00 CEST)

Results Announcement

End of November 2026

Applicants are encouraged to begin preparing their proposals early and secure faculty endorsements well before the deadline.

Why Apply for the CYD Doctoral Fellowship?

This fellowship offers far more than financial support.

Benefits include:

  • Access to Switzerland’s world-class research environment.
  • Collaboration with leading cybersecurity experts.
  • Opportunities to work on real-world cyber-defence challenges.
  • Professional mentorship from CYD Campus researchers.
  • International academic visibility.
  • Conference participation and global networking.
  • Career advancement in cybersecurity, defence, AI, and data science sectors.
  • Opportunity to contribute to national and international cyber resilience.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should:

  1. Carefully review the official application guidelines.
  2. Explore the application toolkit provided by the programme.
  3. Prepare all required documentation.
  4. Ensure all materials are written in English.
  5. Submit applications through the official online submission platform before the deadline.

Applicants are strongly advised to identify potential supervisors early and discuss project ideas before submitting their proposals.

Apply Here

Final Thoughts

The CYD Doctoral Fellowships 2026 represent one of the most prestigious cybersecurity and cyber-defence PhD funding opportunities in Europe. By combining salary support, research funding, conference resources, mentorship, and access to Switzerland’s leading innovation ecosystem, the programme empowers outstanding researchers to develop transformative solutions for the digital security challenges of the future.

For aspiring doctoral researchers interested in cybersecurity, artificial intelligence, data science, digital trust, and cyber-defence, this fellowship offers an unparalleled opportunity to advance both academic excellence and global impact.

For more information, visit the official website.

Discover more global fellowship opportunities on OFY, VISIT HERE

The post Cyber-Defence Doctoral Fellowships 2026 in Switzerland: Fully Funded Cyber-Defence PhD Opportunity for International Researchers appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Africa CDC Mental Health Leadership Programme (MHLP) Short Course 2026 in Nigeria: Leadership and Advocacy Training for Emerging Mental Health Leaders Across Africa
item.dc:creator: admin
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) Mental Health Leadership Programme (MHLP) Short Course 2026, a flagship leadership and advocacy training initiative designed to strengthen mental health systems, policies, advocacy efforts, and leadership capacity across Africa. The 2026 edition of the MHLP Short Course will take place […]

The post Africa CDC Mental Health Leadership Programme (MHLP) Short Course 2026 in Nigeria: Leadership and Advocacy Training for Emerging Mental Health Leaders Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Africa Centres for Disease Control and Prevention (Africa CDC) Mental Health Leadership Programme (MHLP) Short Course 2026, a flagship leadership and advocacy training initiative designed to strengthen mental health systems, policies, advocacy efforts, and leadership capacity across Africa.

The 2026 edition of the MHLP Short Course will take place at the prestigious University of Ibadan in Nigeria from 3–14 August 2026. The programme is calling for applications from emerging leaders, professionals, policymakers, advocates, healthcare workers, and mental health enthusiasts from African Union Member States who are committed to advancing mental health services and promoting the rights and wellbeing of people living with mental health conditions.

As mental health continues to gain recognition as a critical public health and development priority across Africa, the Africa CDC Mental Health Leadership Programme seeks to empower a new generation of leaders capable of driving reforms, combating stigma, influencing policy, and strengthening mental health systems throughout the continent.

About the Africa CDC Mental Health Leadership Programme (MHLP)

The Africa CDC Mental Health Leadership Programme is a flagship initiative established to support the development of strong mental health leadership across African countries.

The programme focuses on:

  • Strengthening mental health systems
  • Promoting mental health advocacy
  • Supporting policy development and reform
  • Building leadership capacity
  • Advancing mental health rights
  • Reducing stigma and discrimination
  • Encouraging evidence-based mental health interventions

Through specialized training and networking opportunities, the programme aims to equip participants with the knowledge, skills, and confidence needed to influence positive change within their organizations, communities, and countries.

About the Mental Health Leadership and Advocacy Programme (mhLAP) Short Course

The Mental Health Leadership and Advocacy Programme (mhLAP) Short Course was established in 2010 and has become one of Africa’s leading capacity-building programmes for mental health advocates and professionals.

The course is specifically designed to prepare participants for informed mental health advocacy and leadership by providing practical tools and strategies to address mental health challenges in low- and middle-income countries (LMICs), particularly within African contexts.

The programme emphasizes the importance of:

  • Leadership development
  • Mental health advocacy
  • Anti-stigma campaigns
  • Mental health policy reform
  • Legislative development
  • Human rights protection
  • Community engagement
  • Health system strengthening

Participants will gain valuable insights into how mental health intersects with public health, social development, human rights, and economic growth.

Programme Dates and Location

Course Dates

3 August 2026 – 14 August 2026

Duration

Two weeks

Course Format

Residential programme

Host Institution

University of Ibadan

Location

Ibadan, Nigeria

The residential format allows participants to engage fully in intensive learning sessions, practical workshops, networking activities, peer learning, and collaborative discussions throughout the programme.

Programme Objectives

The MHLP Short Course seeks to develop a cadre of mental health leaders who can influence positive change at local, national, and regional levels.

The programme aims to:

  1. Strengthen participants’ leadership skills.
  2. Improve understanding of mental health systems.
  3. Enhance mental health advocacy capacity.
  4. Support anti-stigma and awareness initiatives.
  5. Promote policy and legislative reform.
  6. Advance human rights for people with mental health conditions.
  7. Build networks among mental health stakeholders across Africa.
  8. Support innovative approaches to mental health service delivery.
  9. Encourage evidence-based policy development.
  10. Strengthen collaboration among governments, civil society, healthcare providers, and service users.

What Participants Will Learn

Participants will be exposed to a wide range of topics relevant to mental health leadership and advocacy.

Areas of learning may include:

Mental Health Leadership

  • Leadership theories and practice
  • Strategic planning
  • Organizational leadership
  • Change management

Advocacy and Communication

  • Advocacy strategies
  • Public awareness campaigns
  • Stakeholder engagement
  • Strategic communication

Mental Health Policy and Legislation

  • Policy analysis
  • Legislative frameworks
  • Mental health governance
  • Health systems strengthening

Human Rights and Mental Health

  • Rights-based approaches
  • Inclusion and participation
  • Protection of vulnerable groups
  • Human rights advocacy

Stigma Reduction

  • Anti-stigma interventions
  • Community engagement approaches
  • Public education campaigns

Mental Health Systems Development

  • Service delivery models
  • Workforce development
  • Financing and sustainability
  • Integrated mental healthcare

Who Should Apply?

The programme welcomes applications from individuals who are passionate about improving mental health outcomes across Africa.

Applications are encouraged from:

Policymakers

Government officials and policymakers working on health, social welfare, education, or related sectors.

Politicians

Political leaders interested in advancing mental health legislation and reforms.

Mental Health Professionals

Including:

  • Psychiatrists
  • Psychologists
  • Mental health nurses
  • Social workers
  • Counsellors
  • Therapists

Healthcare Workers

Professionals working in health systems who want to strengthen mental health services.

Service Users

Individuals with lived experience of mental health conditions who are involved in advocacy and community initiatives.

Caregivers

People supporting individuals living with mental health conditions.

Civil Society Organizations

Staff and leaders of:

  • Non-governmental organizations (NGOs)
  • Community-based organizations
  • Advocacy groups
  • Youth organizations

Mental Health Enthusiasts

Individuals with innovative ideas and projects aimed at strengthening mental health systems and improving mental health outcomes in Africa.

Africa CDC Scholarship Opportunities

Applicants will be asked whether they intend to apply for an Africa CDC scholarship to support their participation in the programme.

Scholarship opportunities may be available to selected participants based on programme funding and eligibility requirements.

Self-Funding Costs

For applicants who are not receiving scholarship support, participation fees apply.

Nigerian-Based Participants

Self-funded participants based in Nigeria will pay:

  • 5,000 Nigerian Naira

Participants from African Countries and LMICs

Self-funded participants from other African countries and low- and middle-income countries will pay:

  • USD 1,000

Participants from Countries Outside Africa and LMICs

Self-funded participants from countries outside Africa and LMICs will pay:

  • USD 1,500

Applicants should indicate their intended funding source during the application process.

Application Requirements

Applicants will be required to provide:

Personal Information

  • Full name as it appears on passport
  • Email address
  • Alternative email address
  • Country of residence
  • Age group
  • Gender
  • Organization
  • Occupation

Written Responses

Applicants must answer the following questions:

Personal Statement (200 Words)

Tell us about yourself and your work related to mental health.

Community Impact Statement (350 Words)

Explain how participation in the course will benefit your organization and community.

Documentation

Applicants must upload:

  • A valid passport copy (PDF format)

Attendance Commitment

Selected participants must confirm availability for the entire programme period.

Important note:

Partial attendance is not permitted.

All selected participants must attend the full course from 3–14 August 2026.

Application Deadline

26 June 2026

 

APPLY HERE

Why This Programme Matters

Mental health remains one of the most under-resourced areas of public health across many African countries. Limited funding, workforce shortages, stigma, policy gaps, and inadequate services continue to affect millions of people.

The Africa CDC Mental Health Leadership Programme seeks to address these challenges by building a network of skilled leaders who can:

  • Influence policy
  • Drive reform
  • Strengthen services
  • Promote inclusion
  • Protect human rights
  • Advance community-based solutions

The programme recognizes that sustainable change requires strong leadership and coordinated advocacy at every level.

Benefits of Participating

Participants can expect to gain:

  • Advanced leadership skills
  • Practical advocacy knowledge
  • Policy development expertise
  • Regional networking opportunities
  • Exposure to leading mental health experts
  • Collaboration opportunities across Africa
  • Enhanced capacity to influence change
  • Improved ability to design and implement mental health initiatives
  • Stronger understanding of rights-based mental healthcare

Visit HERE to learn more about the Africa CDC Mental Health Leadership Programme (MHLP) Short Course in Nigeria.

Conclusion

The Africa CDC Mental Health Leadership Programme (MHLP) Short Course 2026 presents an outstanding opportunity for emerging leaders and mental health stakeholders across Africa to strengthen their advocacy, leadership, and policy engagement skills. Through intensive training, peer learning, networking, and practical leadership development, participants will gain the tools needed to contribute meaningfully to stronger mental health systems and improved mental health outcomes across the continent. For individuals committed to advancing mental health rights, reducing stigma, influencing policy reforms, and improving services in their communities, the MHLP Short Course offers a powerful platform to develop the expertise and networks necessary to create lasting impact throughout Africa.

Discover more global Fellowship, African and Health opportunities on Opportunities for Youth (OFY).

The post Africa CDC Mental Health Leadership Programme (MHLP) Short Course 2026 in Nigeria: Leadership and Advocacy Training for Emerging Mental Health Leaders Across Africa appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: ICCROM Programme Support Specialist Roster 2026: Global Opportunity for Programme Development and International Development Professionals
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The International Centre for the Study of the Preservation and Restoration of Cultural Property (ICCROM) has officially opened applications for its Programme Support Specialist Roster 2026, providing an exceptional opportunity for qualified professionals worldwide to contribute to international programmes, project development, donor engagement, partnerships, and cultural heritage initiatives. This global call seeks experienced consultants who […]

The post ICCROM Programme Support Specialist Roster 2026: Global Opportunity for Programme Development and International Development Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Centre for the Study of the Preservation and Restoration of Cultural Property (ICCROM) has officially opened applications for its Programme Support Specialist Roster 2026, providing an exceptional opportunity for qualified professionals worldwide to contribute to international programmes, project development, donor engagement, partnerships, and cultural heritage initiatives.

This global call seeks experienced consultants who can support ICCROM’s expanding Programme of Work through flexible, on-demand assignments. Successful candidates will be placed on a roster that remains valid for two years and may be called upon based on organizational priorities, funding availability, technical requirements, and consultant availability.

For professionals with experience in programme development, project management, donor relations, fundraising, monitoring and evaluation, and international cooperation, this roster presents a unique opportunity to collaborate with one of the world’s leading organizations dedicated to the preservation and restoration of cultural heritage.

Application Deadline: 17 June 2026 (11:59 PM CEST)

About ICCROM

The International Centre for the Study of the Preservation and Restoration of Cultural Property (ICCROM) is an intergovernmental organization headquartered in Rome, Italy. It works globally to promote the conservation and protection of cultural heritage through research, training, advisory services, partnerships, and capacity-building initiatives.

As ICCROM continues to expand its strategic programmes and partnerships, the organization is creating a roster of Programme Support Specialists who can provide technical and operational support across various projects and initiatives.

Overview of the Programme Support Specialist Roster

The Programme Support Specialist roster is designed to provide ICCROM with rapid access to highly qualified consultants who can support programme development and implementation activities whenever needed.

Key details include:

  • Position: Programme Support Specialist
  • Contract Type: Individual Engagement Contract
  • Location: Rome, Italy, with possible travel as required
  • Roster Validity: Two years, renewable based on organizational needs
  • Compensation: Daily rate determined according to qualifications, experience, and assignment requirements
  • Application Deadline: 17 June 2026 (11:59 PM CEST)
  • Eligibility: Global

It is important to note that inclusion on the roster does not constitute employment and does not guarantee assignment. Consultants may be engaged when specific organizational needs arise.

Key Responsibilities of Programme Support Specialists

Selected consultants may support a wide range of programme and project activities under the supervision of ICCROM staff members and in collaboration with relevant organizational units.

Responsibilities may include:

1. Programme and Project Development

Consultants may:

  • Support the identification of new programme opportunities.
  • Contribute to project design and development.
  • Assist in creating funding proposals aligned with ICCROM’s Strategic Plan 2026–2031.
  • Help ensure projects align with donor priorities and organizational goals.

2. Programme Implementation Support

Responsibilities may include:

  • Supporting ongoing programme delivery.
  • Assisting with project implementation activities.
  • Coordinating project-related processes and communications.
  • Helping ensure programme objectives are achieved efficiently.

3. Donor Mapping and Funding Opportunity Identification

Consultants may be expected to:

  • Conduct donor research and mapping exercises.
  • Identify potential funding opportunities.
  • Analyze funding trends and partnership prospects.
  • Maintain funding pipelines for future programme development.

4. Proposal Development and Grant Support

Selected experts may assist in preparing:

  • Concept notes
  • Project proposals
  • Logical frameworks
  • Budgets
  • Presentations
  • Donor submissions

This role is particularly attractive for professionals with strong proposal-writing and grants-management experience.

5. Stakeholder and Partnership Engagement

Programme Support Specialists may:

  • Facilitate consultations with stakeholders.
  • Support coordination meetings.
  • Engage with governments and public institutions.
  • Collaborate with foundations and civil society organizations.
  • Strengthen strategic partnerships.

6. Monitoring, Evaluation and Reporting

Consultants may support:

  • Monitoring and evaluation activities.
  • Data collection and analysis.
  • Lessons-learned exercises.
  • Knowledge management initiatives.
  • Donor reporting processes.

7. Communication and Visibility Activities

Additional responsibilities may include:

  • Drafting briefing notes.
  • Preparing talking points.
  • Supporting communication materials.
  • Contributing to programme visibility initiatives.

8. Events, Missions and Coordination

Depending on assignment requirements, consultants may:

  • Support field missions.
  • Participate in partnership initiatives.
  • Assist in organizing meetings and events.
  • Coordinate project-related activities.

Required Educational Qualifications

Applicants should possess:

Essential Qualification

  • An advanced university degree (Master’s degree or equivalent) in:
    • International Development
    • Political Science
    • International Relations
    • Economics
    • Cultural Diplomacy
