    • Related disciplines

Preferred Qualification

Additional academic background in:

  • Heritage Conservation
  • Heritage Studies
  • Related cultural heritage fields

will be considered an advantage.

Required Professional Experience

ICCROM welcomes applications from both junior-level and mid-level professionals.

Junior-Level Consultants

Applicants should have:

  • Between one and three years of progressively responsible professional experience.

Mid-Level Consultants

Applicants should have:

  • Between three and seven years of progressively responsible professional experience.

Relevant experience may include:

  • Programme development
  • Project implementation support
  • Donor relations
  • Fundraising
  • Strategic planning
  • Partnerships
  • Monitoring and evaluation
  • Grant management
  • Proposal writing

Skills and Competencies

Successful candidates should demonstrate:

Strong Writing and Communication Skills

Applicants must be able to:

  • Draft reports effectively.
  • Prepare project proposals.
  • Write briefing materials.
  • Produce concise and professional documentation.

Research and Analytical Capacity

Candidates should be able to:

  • Conduct background research.
  • Analyze complex information.
  • Synthesize findings from multiple sources.

Project Management Knowledge

Understanding of:

  • Project cycle management
  • Results-based management principles
  • Monitoring and evaluation frameworks

is highly valuable.

Interpersonal and Teamwork Skills

ICCROM seeks professionals who can:

  • Work effectively in multicultural environments.
  • Build positive stakeholder relationships.
  • Collaborate across diverse teams.

Organizational Skills

Candidates should demonstrate:

  • Ability to manage multiple assignments.
  • Capacity to work under tight deadlines.
  • Strong planning and coordination skills.

Technical Skills

Applicants should possess proficiency in:

  • Microsoft Word
  • Microsoft Excel
  • Microsoft PowerPoint

Knowledge of the cultural heritage sector will be an added advantage.

Language Requirements

Mandatory

  • Fluency in English (written and spoken)

Highly Desirable

Knowledge of:

  • French
  • Any other official United Nations language

will strengthen an application.

Selection Process

ICCROM will evaluate applicants based on the qualifications and competencies outlined in the call.

The selection process may include:

  1. Application screening
  2. Skills assessments or tests
  3. Competency-based interviews
  4. Final roster placement

Only candidates who meet the required qualifications may be included in the roster.

Required Application Documents

Applicants must submit the following documents in PDF format:

1. Motivation Letter

A well-written motivation letter explaining:

  • Relevant experience
  • Professional achievements
  • Interest in the role
  • Suitability for the roster

2. Updated Curriculum Vitae (CV)

The CV must:

  • Be written in English
  • Reflect current qualifications and experience
  • Highlight relevant programme and project work

Applications missing any required documents will not be considered.

How to Apply

Interested applicants should send their applications in English to:

Email: rosters@iccrom.org

Important Application Instructions

  • Use “Programme Support Specialist” as the email subject line.
  • Submit all required documents in PDF format.
  • Ensure all materials are complete before submission.
  • No modifications can be made after submission.

Important Notes for Applicants

Before applying, candidates should note the following:

  • Inclusion on the roster does not guarantee employment.
  • Consultants will be contacted based on organizational requirements.
  • Funding availability may influence engagement opportunities.
  • Technical suitability and consultant availability will be considered for assignments.
  • All applications will be treated confidentially.

ICCROM’s Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

ICCROM is committed to maintaining the highest standards of efficiency, competence, and integrity.

The organization actively promotes:

  • Gender equality
  • Workforce diversity
  • Geographic representation
  • Cultural inclusion
  • Equal opportunities

ICCROM also maintains a zero-tolerance policy against all forms of harassment and strives to build a diverse and inclusive professional environment.

For qualified professionals seeking international consulting opportunities in programme management, donor relations, project development, fundraising, and cultural heritage, the ICCROM Programme Support Specialist Roster 2026 offers a valuable pathway to contribute to impactful global initiatives while working alongside a respected international organization.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Job Opportunities on OFY here.

The post ICCROM Programme Support Specialist Roster 2026: Global Opportunity for Programme Development and International Development Professionals appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Join the Children’s Commissioner for England Youth Advisory Board on Artificial Intelligence in Education 2026–2027
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
Help Shape the Future of Artificial Intelligence in Education Across England The Children’s Commissioner for England is inviting passionate and forward-thinking young people to become members of a newly established Youth Advisory Board focused on Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Education. This exciting opportunity gives young people a direct platform to share their experiences, perspectives, and […]

The post Join the Children’s Commissioner for England Youth Advisory Board on Artificial Intelligence in Education 2026–2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Help Shape the Future of Artificial Intelligence in Education Across England

The Children’s Commissioner for England is inviting passionate and forward-thinking young people to become members of a newly established Youth Advisory Board focused on Artificial Intelligence (AI) in Education. This exciting opportunity gives young people a direct platform to share their experiences, perspectives, and ideas about how AI is influencing learning, teaching, and education across England.

As AI technologies continue to transform classrooms, learning tools, and educational experiences, it is increasingly important that young people have a voice in the conversations that shape future policies. The Youth Advisory Board has been created to ensure that policymakers hear directly from the young people who are most affected by these changes.

Through regular meetings, discussions, and collaborative engagement with policymakers, board members will play a meaningful role in helping the Department for Education understand the realities of young people’s experiences with AI and contribute to recommendations that could influence educational policy for years to come.

About the Youth Advisory Board

The Youth Advisory Board is a group of young people brought together to provide valuable insights into the opportunities and challenges associated with Artificial Intelligence in education.

Members of the board will have the opportunity to:

  • Share their personal experiences of using AI in educational settings.
  • Discuss how AI is affecting learning, teaching, assessment, and student wellbeing.
  • Highlight concerns, opportunities, and recommendations regarding AI in schools and colleges.
  • Contribute to discussions that may influence future educational policies.
  • Ensure that the voices of young people remain at the center of decisions about educational technology.

The initiative recognizes that young people are among the most active users of emerging technologies and therefore have unique insights that can help shape effective and responsible AI policies.

Benefits of Joining the Youth Advisory Board

Successful participants will enjoy several valuable benefits, including:

1. Influence National Education Policy

Members will have a direct opportunity to inform discussions and policy recommendations related to Artificial Intelligence in Education. Their experiences and opinions will help policymakers better understand how AI is impacting young people.

2. Develop Leadership Skills

Participation in the board will help members strengthen essential skills such as:

  • Public speaking
  • Communication
  • Critical thinking
  • Policy engagement
  • Teamwork
  • Problem-solving
  • Advocacy

3. Collaborate with Other Young Leaders

Participants will join a diverse group of young people from across England who are passionate about education, technology, and creating positive change.

4. Gain Exposure to Emerging Technologies

Board members will deepen their understanding of Artificial Intelligence and its growing role within education systems.

5. Make a Lasting Impact

The recommendations and insights generated by the board may contribute to future decisions that affect children and young people throughout England.

Meeting Structure and Commitment

Successful applicants should be prepared to actively participate throughout the duration of the program.

Key Commitments Include:

Introductory In-Person Meeting

  • Attend an introductory face-to-face meeting in London during July 2026 (exact date to be confirmed).

Regular Advisory Board Meetings

  • Meet every six weeks with fellow advisory board members.
  • Participate in meetings until December 2027.

Hybrid Participation Format

The advisory board will use a combination of in-person and online engagement:

  • Four meetings will be held in person.
  • Remaining meetings will be conducted online.

This structure ensures meaningful collaboration while allowing flexibility for participants from different parts of England.

Why Artificial Intelligence in Education Matters

Artificial Intelligence is rapidly becoming part of everyday educational experiences. Students increasingly encounter AI-powered learning platforms, educational assistants, assessment tools, and digital learning resources.

The Children’s Commissioner for England recognizes that young people are uniquely positioned to provide feedback on:

  • How AI supports learning.
  • Potential risks and challenges associated with AI use.
  • Fairness and accessibility concerns.
  • Privacy and safety considerations.
  • The future role of technology in education.

The Youth Advisory Board provides a valuable opportunity for young people to contribute to these important discussions and ensure that educational policies reflect real-world experiences.

Eligibility Requirements

To be eligible for the Youth Advisory Board, applicants must:

  • Live in England.
  • Be between 16 and 18 years old in July 2026.
  • Be willing to participate in both online and in-person meetings.
  • Have an interest in discussing Artificial Intelligence and its role in education.
  • Be prepared to contribute their views and experiences throughout the advisory period.

Deadline

No specific application deadline has been announced. Interested applicants are encouraged to apply as early as possible once applications are available.

APPLY HERE

Final Thoughts

The Children’s Commissioner for England Youth Advisory Board on Artificial Intelligence in Education offers an exceptional opportunity for young people to influence national conversations about the future of education. By sharing their experiences, insights, and recommendations, participants can help ensure that Artificial Intelligence develops in ways that genuinely benefit children and young people across England.

Young people who are passionate about technology, education, and creating positive change should consider applying and becoming part of this important initiative.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Join the Children’s Commissioner for England Youth Advisory Board on Artificial Intelligence in Education 2026–2027 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
